fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground. Australia won by 45 runs.
India won its first Cricket World Cup in 1983, defeating the West Indies by 43 runs at Lord's under the captaincy of Kapil Dev.
Sachin Tendulkar holds the record for the most international centuries in cricket history, with 100 centuries across Test and ODI formats.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final at Montevideo.
Lionel Messi lifted his first World Cup trophy in 2022 when Argentina defeated France on penalties after a 3-3 draw in regulation and extra time.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times, in 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its first title in 2022, defeating Indonesia 3-0.
PV Sindhu became the first Indian woman to win an Olympic silver medal in badminton at the 2016 Rio Olympics.
The Uber Cup is the women's equivalent of the Thomas Cup and has been dominated historically by China.
Novak Djokovic holds the record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles in tennis history.
The Wimbledon Championships, first held in 1877, is the oldest tennis tournament in the world and the only major still played on grass.
The NBA was founded in 1946 as the Basketball Association of America and adopted the name National Basketball Association in 1949.
Michael Jordan won six NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls in the 1990s and was named Finals MVP in each of those six title runs.
